structural_rules.description: Defines the containment hierarchy of AWS logical groupings.
hierarchy: Availability Zone (within Region, NOT within VPC)
VPC (spans across AZs, drawn inside Region but NOT encapsulating AZs)
Subnet (within VPC, visually overlapping with AZ boundaries)
Resource (must always be placed inside a Subnet — no floating icons allowed)
notes: VPC must NOT visually encapsulate AZ containers.
AZ boundaries are indicated by dashed lines spanning vertically across all subnets in that zone.
Every resource icon MUST reside inside a named Subnet group.
Resources that are logically shared across AZs (e.g. ALB, IGW) MUST be drawn OUTSIDE AZ containers but INSIDE the VPC or Region boundary. Do NOT place them inside any single AZ.

subnet_vertical_order.description: When multiple subnet tiers coexist within an AZ column, stack them in this vertical order (top to bottom).

multi_az_alignment.description: Subnets of the same tier across different AZs must be horizontally aligned.
multi_az_alignment.rule: Each AZ column must mirror the subnet structure of the other AZ columns.

subnet_sizing.description: Subnet containers must be sized proportionally to the resources they contain. Do NOT create oversized subnets with excessive whitespace.
rules: A subnet containing a single icon should be approximately 180px wide and 140px tall.
A subnet containing two icons side by side should be approximately 300px wide and 140px tall.
Add roughly 60px of padding around icons within the subnet boundary.
The overall diagram width should be kept compact. Avoid unnecessary horizontal stretching.

shared_resource_placement.description: Resources shared across AZs must not be duplicated per AZ in the diagram.
rules: ALB is a single logical resource that spans AZs. Draw ONE ALB icon outside AZ containers, inside the VPC or between AZ columns.
IGW is a regional resource. Draw it outside the VPC, within the Region boundary.
NAT Gateway is AZ-specific and should be drawn inside its respective Public Subnet.

layout_rules.legend_placement: Bottom-left of the diagram, outside all group containers.

rules: Every resource icon must have a label directly below it.
Labels must include the resource type and its role. Format: '<ServiceName> (<Role>)'. Example: 'RDS (Primary)', 'RDS (Standby / Multi-AZ)', 'EC2 (App Server)'.
Every subnet must be labeled with its type and purpose. Format: '<Visibility> Subnet (<Purpose>)'. Example: 'Private Subnet (DB)', 'Public Subnet (ALB)'.
Every connection line must be explainable by the Legend. Unlabeled or undefined line types are prohibited.

arrow_rules.general_principle: Arrows represent the direction of data flow (source → destination). Arrow direction must always match the actual direction data travels, not the direction of a request.
arrow_rules.completeness: Every arrow MUST have a clearly identifiable source icon and destination icon. Arrows must not start or end in empty space. All intermediate hops in a traffic path must be explicitly drawn as separate arrows (e.g., User → IGW → ALB, not User → ALB).
arrow_rules.one_meaning_per_line: Each line type (color + style combination) must carry exactly one semantic meaning. Never combine two meanings into a single line definition.
arrow_rules.bidirectional_usage: Use bidirectional arrows (startArrow + endArrow) only when data genuinely flows in both directions simultaneously (e.g., synchronous replication with acknowledgement).

external_traffic.description: Traffic entering from outside the VPC (e.g., from the internet user to IGW, or IGW to ALB).
external_traffic.direction: Unidirectional: Source → Destination (e.g., User → IGW, then IGW → ALB as separate arrows)

internal_traffic_write.description: Write or read/write traffic between internal resources.
internal_traffic_write.direction: Originating resource → target resource (e.g., App Server → RDS Primary)

internal_traffic_read.direction: Originating resource → target resource (e.g., App Server → RDS Read Replica)

outbound_nat.description: Outbound internet traffic from private subnets routed via NAT Gateway. Must be drawn as a complete path: Private Resource → NAT Gateway → IGW → Internet.
outbound_nat.direction: Unidirectional. Each hop drawn as a separate arrow of the same style.

sync_replication.description: Synchronous replication between two nodes (e.g., RDS Multi-AZ Primary ↔ Standby). Data is written to both simultaneously.

async_replication.description: Asynchronous replication from a source to a replica. Data flows one-way with a lag.

resource_icons.description: All icons use the official mxgraph.aws4 shape library. Do NOT use generic or non-AWS icons (e.g., CPU/chip shapes) for any AWS service.

rds_opacity_convention.description: RDS icons use opacity to visually convey the role of each instance.
rds_opacity_convention.standby: opacity=40 (strongly faded) — passive failover target, no direct access

external_traffic.meaning: Traffic entering the system from outside the VPC (e.g., User → IGW, IGW → ALB)

internal_traffic_write.meaning: Write or read/write traffic between internal resources (e.g., App Server → RDS Primary)

internal_traffic_read.meaning: Read-only traffic between internal resources (e.g., App Server → RDS Read Replica)

outbound_nat.meaning: Outbound internet traffic via NAT Gateway. Each hop (Private Resource → NAT GW → IGW) drawn separately.

sync_replication.meaning: Synchronous replication (bidirectional). Used for RDS Multi-AZ Primary ↔ Standby.

async_replication.meaning: Asynchronous replication (unidirectional). Used for RDS Read Replica. Arrow points FROM Primary TO Replica.

legend_required_entries.description: The Legend group must include an entry for every line type used in the diagram. Unused line types may be omitted.
examples: Orange (solid): Internal write traffic (App → RDS Primary)
Blue (solid): Internal read traffic (App → RDS Read Replica)
Blue (dashed, bidirectional): Synchronous replication (Multi-AZ Primary ↔ Standby)
Gray (dashed): Outbound via NAT Gateway → IGW → Internet

common_mistakes_to_avoid: Do NOT place VPC as a container that wraps AZ groups.
Do NOT leave any resource icon outside of a named Subnet (except shared resources like ALB and IGW which follow shared_resource_placement rules).
Do NOT use a single line type to represent two different meanings.
Do NOT draw replication arrows in the wrong direction — always point from source to destination of data.
Do NOT place ALB inside a single AZ subnet. ALB is a cross-AZ resource and must be drawn outside AZ containers.
Do NOT draw an arrow that starts or ends in empty space. Every arrow must connect two explicit resource icons.
Do NOT represent a multi-hop path (e.g., Private EC2 → NAT GW → IGW → Internet) as a single arrow. Draw each hop as a separate arrow.
Do NOT leave a labeled subnet empty without a comment or placeholder indicating its purpose.
Do NOT use non-AWS icons (e.g., generic CPU/chip icons) to represent AWS services. Always use the official mxgraph.aws4 icon shapes.
Do NOT make subnet containers excessively large. Size subnets to fit their contents with ~60px padding only.
